inline_review:
commit 992928cd3b6d108008d76e477c0feb870989a2c9
Author: Baolin Wang <baolin.wang@linux.alibaba.com>

mm: change to return bool for the MMU notifier's young flag check

This commit updates the MMU notifier young flag check functions to return a
boolean instead of an integer to make the code's intention clearer.

> diff --git a/mm/mmu_notifier.c b/mm/mmu_notifier.c
> index 2502474b83b6..3e3e7e727ba2 100644
> --- a/mm/mmu_notifier.c
> +++ b/mm/mmu_notifier.c
> @@ -364,12 +364,11 @@ void __mmu_notifier_release(struct mm_struct *mm)
[ ... ]
> -int __mmu_notifier_clear_flush_young(struct mm_struct *mm,
> -					unsigned long start,
> -					unsigned long end)
> +bool __mmu_notifier_clear_flush_young(struct mm_struct *mm,
> +		unsigned long start, unsigned long end)
>  {
>  	struct mmu_notifier *subscription;
> -	int young = 0, id;
> +	bool young = false, id;

Does this inadvertently change the type of id from int to bool?

The id variable is used to store the return value of srcu_read_lock(),
which returns an int. While the SRCU implementation currently returns
indices 0 or 1, storing this opaque token in a bool breaks the API
contract.

Would it be safer to separate id into its own int declaration here, as
well as in the similar changes made to __mmu_notifier_clear_young() and
__mmu_notifier_test_young()?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The SRCU lock index variable `id` is inadvertently declared as a `bool` instead of an `int`.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"In `__mmu_notifier_clear_flush_young`, `__mmu_notifier_clear_young`, and `__mmu_notifier_test_young` inside `mm/mmu_notifier.c`, the variable declaration `int young = 0, id;` was changed to `bool young = false, id;`. This inadvertently changes the type of `id` to `bool`. The variable `id` is used to store the return value of `srcu_read_lock(&srcu)`, which is later passed back to `srcu_read_unlock(&srcu, id)`. The SRCU API explicitly specifies that `srcu_read_lock` returns an `int` (an opaque index token). Storing this integer in a `bool` causes an implicit type conversion. While the SRCU implementation currently happens to return indices `0` or `1` (which means the boolean conversion is lossless today and won't cause immediate runtime failures), relying on `bool` truncation violates the SRCU API contract. The variable `id` should be separated into its own `int id;` declaration to properly handle the opaque token."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 992928cd3b6d108008d76e477c0feb870989a2c9
Author: Baolin Wang <baolin.wang@linux.alibaba.com>

mm: change to return bool for the MMU notifier's young flag check

This commit updates the MMU notifier young flag check functions to return a
boolean instead of an integer to make the code's intention clearer.

> diff --git a/mm/mmu_notifier.c b/mm/mmu_notifier.c

> --- a/mm/mmu_notifier.c
> +++ b/mm/mmu_notifier.c
> @@ -364,12 +364,11 @@ void __mmu_notifier_release(struct mm_struct *mm)
[ ... ]
> -int __mmu_notifier_clear_flush_young(struct mm_struct *mm,
> -					unsigned long start,
> -					unsigned long end)
> +bool __mmu_notifier_clear_flush_young(struct mm_struct *mm,
> +		unsigned long start, unsigned long end)
>  {
>  	struct mmu_notifier *subscription;
> -	int young = 0, id;
> +	bool young = false, id;

Does this inadvertently change the type of id from int to bool?

The id variable is used to store the return value of srcu_read_lock(),
which returns an int. While the SRCU implementation currently returns
indices 0 or 1, storing this opaque token in a bool breaks the API
contract.

Would it be safer to separate id into its own int declaration here, as
well as in the similar changes made to __mmu_notifier_clear_young() and
__mmu_notifier_test_young()?

"}
